body.title: Three Postgres schemas: igor instance, clan, infrastructure
body.result: Three Postgres schemas (instance/clan/infra). Migration m050 moves 38 tables. PGDatabaseProxy search_path per factory. make_infra_proxy() for UC services. DB-level default search_path. 17 tests.
body.description:
Create three Postgres schemas to separate brain, species, and body.

## Schemas

### `instance` schema — Igor's brain state (per-instance)
Moved from public schema. Instance-scoped data:
- ring_memory
- twm_observations (already has instance_id filter)
- tails
- traversal_contexts
- pending_replies
- wg_access_log
- cloud_escalations

### `clan` schema — species knowledge (shared across Igors)
Moved from public schema. Any Igor can read, writes governed:
- memories
- memory_embeddings
- memory_blobs
- interpretive_edges
- reading_list, reading_runs, reading_run_items
- lists
- wg_meta, wg_word_lang, wg_idf, wg_word_docs, wg_cooccur, wg_edges
- blobs, blob_chunks
- traces
- experiment_queue
- active_slate

### `infra` schema — utility closet state (all agents)
Moved from public schema + new tables:
- machines (from machine_manager)
- channel_messages → infra.comms_messages (rename)
- sessions (CC session records)
- slates (CC slate history)
- memory_palace (navigable tree — used by CC and Igor)
- instance_log (boot/shutdown history)
- _migrations
- _cc_index_registry
- NEW: infra.comms_channels (channel definitions)
- NEW: infra.tools (shared tool registry)
- NEW: infra.budget_events (moved from SQLite spend/balance_history)

## Implementation

### Step 1: Create schemas
```sql
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS instance;
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS clan;
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS infra;
```

### Step 2: Migration script
New migration file: `m050_three_schemas.py`
- For each table: `ALTER TABLE public.<table> SET SCHEMA <target>;`
- Order matters: foreign keys (interpretive_edges refs memories) must move together
- Update search_path in db_proxy connection: `SET search_path TO instance, clan, infra, public;`
- This means existing queries work without schema prefixes during transition

### Step 3: db_proxy schema awareness
- `make_home_proxy()` sets search_path to `clan, infra, public` (shared access)
- `make_local_proxy()` sets search_path to `instance, clan, infra, public` (full access)
- New: `make_infra_proxy()` sets search_path to `infra, public` (UC services only)
- Access control via search_path, not row-level security (simple, sufficient)

### Step 4: Budget migration from SQLite
- Move spend, config, balance_history from SQLite (~/.TheIgors/instance/claude_budget.db) to infra schema
- Delete SQLite budget DB after migration verified
- This is the LAST SQLite removal — everything Postgres after this

## Testing
- Migration script runs idempotently (IF NOT EXISTS, check current schema)
- All existing queries work unchanged (search_path handles resolution)
- db_proxy.get_metrics() still works
- cortex operations unchanged
- budget.py reads from Postgres instead of SQLite

## Rollback
- Reverse migration: ALTER TABLE <schema>.<table> SET SCHEMA public;
- search_path revert in db_proxy

## Depends on
- Nothing — this can go first, sets the foundation

## Related
- T-uc-db-proxy-shelf — db_proxy moves to UC after schemas established
